  1466→# =============================================================================
  1467→# JavaScript Code Generator
  1468→# =============================================================================
  1469→
  1470→class JavaScriptCodeGenerator:
  1471→    """Generates JavaScript module code from transformation config."""
  1472→
  1473→    def __init__(self, module_name: str, config: Dict, file_ext: str):
  1474→        self.module_name = module_name
  1475→        self.config = config
  1476→        self.file_ext = file_ext
  1477→
  1478→    def generate(self) -> str:
  1479→        delimiter = ',' if self.file_ext == 'csv' else '\t' if self.file_ext == 'tsv' else None
  1480→        has_stateful = bool(self.config.get('stateful_transforms'))
  1481→        has_neighbor_filters = bool(self.config.get('neighbor_filters'))
  1482→
  1483→        lines = [
  1484→            '/**',
  1485→            ' * Dynamic Preprocessor - Streaming data processor with caching support.',
  1486→            ' * Supports stateful transforms and neighbor-based filtering.',
  1487→            ' */',
  1488→            '',
  1489→            "const fs = require('fs');",
  1490→            "const path = require('path');",
  1491→            "const crypto = require('crypto');",
  1492→            '',
  1493→            'const CONFIG = ' + json.dumps(self.config, indent=2) + ';',
  1494→            '',
  1495→        ]
  1496→
  1497→        # Generate state initialization
  1498→        if has_stateful:
  1499→            lines.extend(self._generate_js_state_init())
  1500→
  1501→        lines.extend([
  1502→            'function parseValue(value) {',
  1503→            "    if (value === '') return null;",
  1504→            "    if (value.toLowerCase() === 'true') return true;",
  1505→            "    if (value.toLowerCase() === 'false') return false;",
  1506→            '    const intVal = parseInt(value, 10);',
  1507→            '    if (!isNaN(intVal) && String(intVal) === value) return intVal;',
  1508→            '    const floatVal = parseFloat(value);',
  1509→            '    if (!isNaN(floatVal)) return floatVal;',
  1510→            '    return value;',
  1511→            '}',
  1512→            '',
  1513→            'function shouldKeepRow(row) {',
  1514→        ])
  1515→        lines.extend(self._generate_filter_logic_js())
  1516→        lines.extend([
  1517→            '    return true;',
  1518→            '}',
  1519→            '',
  1520→            'function transformRow(row, state) {',
  1521→            '    const result = {};',
  1522→        ])
  1523→        lines.extend(self._generate_transform_logic_js())
  1524→
  1525→        if has_stateful:
  1526→            lines.extend(self._generate_stateful_transform_js())
  1527→
  1528→        lines.extend([
  1529→            '    return result;',
  1530→            '}',
  1531→            '',
  1532→        ])
  1533→
  1534→        if has_stateful:
  1535→            lines.extend(self._generate_js_state_methods())
  1536→
  1537→        lines.extend([
  1538→            'function* parseFile(filePath) {',
  1539→        ])
  1540→
  1541→        if self.file_ext in ('csv', 'tsv'):
  1542→            lines.extend([
  1543→                "    const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');",
  1544→                "    const lines = content.trim().split('\\n');",
  1545→                '    if (lines.length === 0) return;',
  1546→                '',
  1547→                "    const headers = lines[0].split('" + delimiter + "');",
  1548→                '    for (let i = 1; i < lines.length; i++) {',
  1549→                '        const line = lines[i].trim();',
  1550→                '        if (!line) continue;',
  1551→                "        const values = line.split('" + delimiter + "');",
  1552→                '        const row = {};',
  1553→                '        for (let j = 0; j < headers.length; j++) {',
  1554→                '            row[headers[j]] = j < values.length ? parseValue(values[j]) : null;',
  1555→                '        }',
  1556→                '        yield row;',
  1557→                '    }',
  1558→            ])
  1559→        elif self.file_ext == 'jsonl':
  1560→            lines.extend([
  1561→                "    const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');",
  1562→                "    const lines = content.trim().split('\\n');",
  1563→                '    for (const line of lines) {',
  1564→                '        if (line.trim()) {',
  1565→                '            yield JSON.parse(line);',
  1566→                '        }',
  1567→                '    }',
  1568→            ])
  1569→        elif self.file_ext == 'json':
  1570→            lines.extend([
  1571→                "    const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');",
  1572→                '    const data = JSON.parse(content);',
  1573→                '    for (const row of data) {',
  1574→                '        yield row;',
  1575→                '    }',
  1576→            ])
  1577→
  1578→        lines.extend([
  1579→            '}',
  1580→            '',
  1581→            'function getCachePath(inputPath, cacheDir) {',
  1582→            '    if (!cacheDir) return null;',
  1583→            "    const hash = crypto.createHash('md5').update(path.resolve(inputPath)).digest('hex');",
  1584→            '    if (!fs.existsSync(cacheDir)) {',
  1585→            "        fs.mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true });",
  1586→            '    }',
  1587→            "    return path.join(cacheDir, `${hash}.json`);",
  1588→            '}',
  1589→            '',
  1590→            'function loadCache(cachePath, state) {',
  1591→            '    if (!cachePath || !fs.existsSync(cachePath)) {',
  1592→            '        return { processedRows: 0, rows: [] };',
  1593→            '    }',
  1594→            "    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(cachePath, 'utf-8'));",
  1595→            '    if (data.state) {',
  1596→            '        restoreState(data.state, state);',
  1597→            '    }',
  1598→            '    return data;',
  1599→            '}',
  1600→            '',
  1601→            'function saveCache(cachePath, state, processorState) {',
  1602→            '    if (!cachePath) return;',
  1603→            '    state.state = getCurrentState(processorState);',
  1604→            '    fs.writeFileSync(cachePath, JSON.stringify(state));',
  1605→            '}',
  1606→            '',
  1607→            'class DynamicPreprocessor {',
  1608→            '    constructor({ buffer, cache_dir = null }) {',
  1609→            '        this.buffer = buffer;',
  1610→            '        this.cacheDir = cache_dir;',
  1611→            '        this.state = this._initState();',
  1612→            '    }',
  1613→            '',
  1614→            '    _initState() {',
  1615→            '        return {',
  1616→        ])
  1617→
  1618→        # Initialize state for stateful transforms
  1619→        if has_stateful:
  1620→            stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
  1621→            for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
  1622→                st_type = st.get('type')
  1623→                if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
  1624→                    lines.append(f'            prefixSum_{i}: 0.0,')
  1625→                elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
  1626→                    lines.append(f'            prefixCount_{i}: 0,')
  1627→                elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
  1628→                    lines.append(f'            window_{i}: [],')
  1629→                    lines.append(f'            windowSize_{i}: {st.get("window_size", 3)},')
  1630→                elif st_type == 'state_machine':
  1631→                    initial = st.get('initial_state', 1)
  1632→                    lines.append(f'            state_{i}: {repr(initial)},')
  1633→
  1634→        lines.extend([
  1635→            '        };',
  1636→            '    }',
  1637→            '',
  1638→            '    process(filePath) {',
  1639→            '        const self = this;',
  1640→            '        return {',
  1641→            '            [Symbol.iterator]: function* () {',
  1642→            '                const cachePath = getCachePath(filePath, self.cacheDir);',
  1643→            '                const cacheState = loadCache(cachePath, self.state);',
  1644→            '                const startRow = cacheState.processedRows || 0;',
  1645→            '',
  1646→            '                let rowCount = 0;',
  1647→            '                let bufferCount = 0;',
  1648→            '                const currentCacheRows = [];',
  1649→            '',
  1650→        ])
  1651→
  1652→        if has_neighbor_filters:
  1653→            lines.extend([
  1654→                '                let pendingRow = null;',
  1655→                '                let pendingInputIdx = null;',
  1656→                '                let prevRow = null;',
  1657→                '',
  1658→            ])
  1659→
  1660→        lines.extend([
  1661→            '                for (const row of parseFile(filePath)) {',
  1662→        ])
  1663→
  1664→        if has_neighbor_filters:
  1665→            lines.extend(self._generate_js_neighbor_filter_logic())
  1666→            # When neighbor filters are active, rows are processed through the pending mechanism
  1667→        else:
  1668→            lines.extend([
  1669→                '                    if (rowCount < startRow) {',
  1670→                '                        rowCount++;',
  1671→                '                        continue;',
  1672→                '                    }',
  1673→                '',
  1674→                '                    if (!shouldKeepRow(row)) {',
  1675→                '                        rowCount++;',
  1676→                '                        cacheState.processedRows = rowCount;',
  1677→                '                        saveCache(cachePath, cacheState, self.state);',
  1678→                '                        continue;',
  1679→                '                    }',
  1680→                '',
  1681→                '                    const transformed = transformRow(row, self.state);',
  1682→                '',
  1683→                '                    if (cachePath) {',
  1684→                '                        currentCacheRows.push(transformed);',
  1685→                '                        bufferCount++;',
  1686→                '',
  1687→                '                        if (bufferCount >= self.buffer) {',
  1688→                '                            cacheState.processedRows = rowCount + 1;',
  1689→                '                            cacheState.rows = currentCacheRows;',
  1690→                '                            saveCache(cachePath, cacheState, self.state);',
  1691→                '                            currentCacheRows.length = 0;',
  1692→                '                            bufferCount = 0;',
  1693→                '                        }',
  1694→                '                    }',
  1695→                '',
  1696→                '                    rowCount++;',
  1697→                '                    yield transformed;',
  1698→            ])
  1699→
  1700→        lines.extend([
  1701→            '                }',
  1702→            '',
  1703→        ])
  1704→
  1705→        # Handle pending row at end
  1706→        if has_neighbor_filters:
  1707→            lines.extend([
  1708→                '                // Handle pending row at end',
  1709→                '                if (pendingRow !== null) {',
  1710→                '                    let keep = true;',
  1711→            ])
  1712→
  1713→            neighbor_filters = self.config.get('neighbor_filters', [])
  1714→            for i, nf in enumerate(neighbor_filters):
  1715→                nf_type = nf.get('type')
  1716→                if nf_type == 'next_row_condition':
  1717→                    col = nf.get('column')
  1718→                    val = self._js_val_str(nf.get('value'))
  1719→                    lines.extend([
  1720→                        f'                    keep = keep && (null === null || pendingRow.next_{col} !== {val});',
  1721→                    ])
  1722→
  1723→            lines.extend([
  1724→                '                    if (keep && shouldKeepRow(pendingRow)) {',
  1725→                '                        const transformed = transformRow(pendingRow, self.state);',
  1726→                '                        if (cachePath) {',
  1727→                '                            currentCacheRows.push(transformed);',
  1728→                '                            cacheState.processedRows = rowCount + 1;',
  1729→                '                            cacheState.rows = currentCacheRows;',
  1730→                '                            saveCache(cachePath, cacheState, self.state);',
  1731→                '                        }',
  1732→                '                        yield transformed;',
  1733→                '                    }',
  1734→                '                }',
  1735→                '',
  1736→            ])
  1737→
  1738→        lines.extend([
  1739→            '                if (cachePath && (bufferCount > 0 || rowCount > startRow)) {',
  1740→            '                    cacheState.processedRows = rowCount;',
  1741→            '                    cacheState.rows = currentCacheRows;',
  1742→            '                    saveCache(cachePath, cacheState, self.state);',
  1743→            '                }',
  1744→            '            }',
  1745→            '        };',
  1746→            '    }',
  1747→            '',
  1748→            '    [Symbol.iterator]() {',
  1749→            "        throw new Error('Call process(filePath) to get an iterator');",
  1750→            '    }',
  1751→            '}',
  1752→            '',
  1753→            'function createPreprocessor(options) {',
  1754→            '    const preprocessor = new DynamicPreprocessor(options);',
  1755→            '    return function(filePath) {',
  1756→            '        return preprocessor.process(filePath);',
  1757→            '    };',
  1758→            '}',
  1759→            '',
  1760→            'module.exports = { DynamicPreprocessor, createPreprocessor };',
  1761→            '',
  1762→        ])
  1763→        return '\n'.join(lines)
  1764→
  1765→    def _generate_js_state_init(self) -> List[str]:
  1766→        """Generate JavaScript state initialization code."""
  1767→        return []
  1768→
  1769→    def _generate_js_state_methods(self) -> List[str]:
  1770→        """Generate JavaScript state save/restore methods."""
  1771→        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
  1772→
  1773→        lines = [
  1774→            'function getCurrentState(state) {',
  1775→            '    return {',
  1776→        ]
  1777→
  1778→        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
  1779→            st_type = st.get('type')
  1780→            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
  1781→                lines.append(f'        prefixSum_{i}: state.prefixSum_{i},')
  1782→            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
  1783→                lines.append(f'        prefixCount_{i}: state.prefixCount_{i},')
  1784→            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
  1785→                lines.append(f'        window_{i}: state.window_{i}.slice(),')
  1786→            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
  1787→                lines.append(f'        state_{i}: state.state_{i},')
  1788→
  1789→        lines.extend([
  1790→            '    };',
  1791→            '}',
  1792→            '',
  1793→            'function restoreState(savedState, state) {',
  1794→        ])
  1795→
  1796→        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
  1797→            st_type = st.get('type')
  1798→            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
  1799→                lines.append(f'    state.prefixSum_{i} = savedState.prefixSum_{i} || 0.0;')
  1800→            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
  1801→                lines.append(f'    state.prefixCount_{i} = savedState.prefixCount_{i} || 0;')
  1802→            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
  1803→                lines.append(f'    state.window_{i} = savedState.window_{i} || [];')
  1804→            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
  1805→                initial = st.get('initial_state', 1)
  1806→                lines.append(f'    state.state_{i} = savedState.state_{i} !== undefined ? savedState.state_{i} : {initial};')
  1807→
  1808→        lines.extend([
  1809→            '}',
  1810→            '',
  1811→        ])
  1812→
  1813→        return lines
  1814→
  1815→    @staticmethod
  1816→    def _js_val_str(val: Any) -> str:
  1817→        if isinstance(val, bool):
  1818→            return 'true' if val else 'false'
  1819→        if isinstance(val, str):
  1820→            return "'" + val + "'"
  1821→        return str(val)
  1822→
  1823→    def _generate_filter_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
  1824→        conditions = self.config.get('filter_conditions', [])
  1825→        lines = []
  1826→        for cond in conditions:
  1827→            col = cond['column']
  1828→            op = cond['operator']
  1829→            val_str = self._js_val_str(cond['value'])
  1830→            if op == '!=':
  1831→                lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] === " + val_str + ") return false;")
  1832→            elif op == '==':
  1833→                lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] !== " + val_str + ") return false;")
  1834→            elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):
  1835→                comp_ops = {'>': '<=', '>=': '<', '<': '>=', '<=': '>'}
  1836→                lines.append("    if (typeof row['" + col + "'] === 'number' && row['" + col + "'] " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ") return false;")
  1837→        return lines
  1838→
  1839→    def _generate_transform_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
  1840→        transforms = self.config.get('column_transforms', {})
  1841→        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
  1842→        stateful_output_cols = {st.get('output_column') for st in stateful_transforms}
  1843→        output_cols = self.config.get('output_columns', [])
  1844→        lines = []
  1845→        for out_col in output_cols:
  1846→            if out_col in stateful_output_cols:
  1847→                continue
  1848→
  1849→            transform = transforms.get(out_col, {'type': 'unknown'})
  1850→            t_type = transform.get('type')
  1851→            source = transform.get('source', out_col)
  1852→            v = "val_" + out_col.replace('-', '_')
  1853→            if t_type in ('identity', 'copy'):
  1854→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = row['" + source + "'];")
  1855→            elif t_type == 'constant':
  1856→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + self._js_val_str(transform['value']) + ";")
  1857→            elif t_type in ('strip', 'lower', 'upper', 'strip_lower', 'strip_upper'):
  1858→                method_map = {'strip': 'trim', 'lower': 'toLowerCase', 'upper': 'toUpperCase',
  1859→                             'strip_lower': 'trim().toLowerCase', 'strip_upper': 'trim().toUpperCase'}
  1860→                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
  1861→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = typeof " + v + " === 'string' ? " + v + "." + method_map[t_type] + "() : " + v + ";")
  1862→            elif t_type == 'add_prefix':
  1863→                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
  1864→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + v + " != null ? '" + transform['prefix'] + "' + String(" + v + ") : null;")
  1865→            elif t_type == 'add_suffix':
  1866→                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
  1867→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + v + " != null ? String(" + v + ") + '" + transform['suffix'] + "' : null;")
  1868→            elif t_type == 'linear':
  1869→                a, b = transform['a'], transform['b']
  1870→                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
  1871→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = typeof " + v + " === 'number' ? " + str(a) + " * " + v + " + " + str(b) + " : null;")
  1872→            elif t_type != 'stateful':
  1873→                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = row['" + out_col + "'];")
  1874→        return lines
  1875→
  1876→    def _generate_stateful_transform_js(self) -> List[str]:
  1877→        """Generate JavaScript code for stateful transforms."""
  1878→        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
  1879→        lines = []
  1880→
  1881→        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
  1882→            st_type = st.get('type')
  1883→            out_col = st.get('output_column')
  1884→            source = st.get('source')
  1885→            a = st.get('a', 1.0)
  1886→            b = st.get('b', 0.0)
  1887→
  1888→            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
  1889→                lines.extend([
  1890→                    "    const val_psum_" + str(i) + " = row['" + str(source) + "'];",
  1891→                    '    if (typeof val_psum_' + str(i) + ' === "number") {',
  1892→                    '        state.prefixSum_' + str(i) + ' += val_psum_' + str(i) + ';',
  1893→                    '    }',
  1894→                    '    result["' + out_col + '"] = ' + str(a) + ' * state.prefixSum_' + str(i) + ' + ' + str(b) + ';',
  1895→                ])
  1896→            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
  1897→                condition = st.get('condition')
  1898→                lines.append("    const val_pcnt_" + str(i) + " = row['" + str(source) + "'];")
  1899→
  1900→                if condition == 'not_null':
  1901→                    lines.extend([
  1902→                        '    if (val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' !== null && val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' !== undefined) {',
  1903→                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
  1904→                        '    }',
  1905→                    ])
  1906→                elif condition == 'positive':
  1907→                    lines.extend([
  1908→                        '    if (typeof val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' === "number" && val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' > 0) {',
  1909→                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
  1910→                        '    }',
  1911→                    ])
  1912→                elif condition == 'negative':
  1913→                    lines.extend([
  1914→                        '    if (typeof val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' === "number" && val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' < 0) {',
  1915→                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
  1916→                        '    }',
  1917→                    ])
  1918→                elif condition == 'true':
  1919→                    lines.extend([
  1920→                        '    if (val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' === true) {',
  1921→                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
  1922→                        '    }',
  1923→                    ])
  1924→                else:
  1925→                    lines.append('    state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;')
  1926→
  1927→                lines.append('    result["' + out_col + '"] = ' + str(a) + ' * state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + ' + ' + str(b) + ';')
  1928→
  1929→            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
  1930→                window_size = st.get('window_size', 3)
  1931→                operation = st.get('operation', 'mean')

content.thinking:
Now let me analyze this file thoroughly for code quality issues according to the 5 rule categories. This is a large file (2133 lines) that generates Python and JavaScript code for data transformation modules.

Let me identify issues:

**1. STRUCTURAL SIMPLIFICATION:**

a) **Duplicate _parse_filter_value and parse_value functions** (lines 56-72 and 354-367) - These are essentially identical. The only difference is the context.

b) **Redundant linear transform checking logic** - `_check_numeric_transform` (467-487), `_try_prefix_sum` (518-567), `_try_window_op` (672-711) all have similar "check if linear relationship exists" logic with a, b calculation. This can be extracted.

c) **Transform logic generation** - `_generate_transform_logic` (1159-1195) and `_generate_transform_logic_js` (1839-1874) are nearly identical in structure. They can share a common model.

d) **Stateful transform generation** - Similar duplication between `_generate_stateful_transform_logic` (1197-1297) and `_generate_stateful_transform_js` (1876-1985).

e) **Filter logic generation** - `_generate_filter_logic` (1086-1112) and `_generate_filter_logic_js` (1823-1837) are similar.

f) **The row matching and scoring logic** - `_row_match_score` (142-160) has scattered conditionals that could be simplified.

g) **_find_distinguishing_conditions** has pass-through pattern - just calls other methods and extends conditions.

**2. SPAGHETTI PREVENTION:**

a) **_infer_filter_conditions** (174-208) - Has a complex nested structure with early returns and flags (`per_row_explains_all`). The logic for checking if per-row conditions explain all drops is convoluted.

b) **_infer_neighbor_filters** (231-307) - Long function with multiple pattern checks, early returns, and nested loops. 

Would benefit from extracting the patterns into separate functions.

c) **_try_state_machine** (713-765) - Complex nested logic with scoring and condition finding.

d) **Main loop generation** - `_generate_main_loop` (1299-1389) and `_generate_neighbor_filter_logic` (1393-1461) have complex nested conditionals and similar code blocks repeated.

**3. ABSTRACTION QUALITY:**

a) **Thin wrappers:** 
   - `parse_tsv` (42-43) just calls `parse_csv` with a different delimiter
   - `get_file_extension` (75-76) is a one-liner that could be inlined

b) **_generate_js_state_init** (1765-1767) - Returns empty list, completely unnecessary

c) **_py_val_str** and **_js_val_str** are nearly identical - could share logic

d) **_check_exact_match** (412-418) and **_check_column_copy** (420-426) are essentially the same function with different column names

**4. SLOP REMOVAL:**

a) **Multi-line docstrings that just restate function names:**
   - Line 26: `"""Parse a CSV/TSV file and return list of row dicts."""` - OK, adds info
   - Line 57: `"""Parse a string value to appropriate Python type."""` - OK
   - Line 80: `"""Parse a data file and return rows and extension."""` - OK
   - Line 98: `"""Infers transformations from input/output sample data."""` - Just restates class name
   - Line 851: `"""Generates Python module code from transformation config."""` - Just restates class name
   - Line 1470: `"""Generates JavaScript module code from transformation config."""` - Just restates class name

I'll focus on simplifying the complex nested logic, removing unnecessary abstractions, and eliminating redundant code. The goal is to improve code readability and maintainability by breaking down complex functions, extracting repeated patterns, and removing unnecessary complexity.

Key actions:
- Extract complex nested logic into separate functions
- Remove thin wrapper functions
- Consolidate similar utility methods
- Simplify docstrings to add meaningful information
- Reduce code duplication across transform and filter logic generation methods

content.thinking:
Now I need to look at the linear relationship checking pattern. It appears in multiple places:
- `_check_numeric_transform` (lines 467-487)
- `_try_prefix_sum` (lines 518-566)
- `_try_prefix_count` (lines 569-624)
- `_try_window_op` (lines 672-711)

All these follow the same pattern:
1. Get x1, x2, y1, y2
2. Check if x1 == x2 (avoid division by zero)
3. Calculate a = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1)
4. Calculate b = y1 - a * x1
5. Verify all points match

Let me extract this into a helper function.

    def _try_infer_stateful_transform(self, out_col: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Try to infer a stateful transform for the output column."""
        if not self.output_rows:
            return None

        out_values = [row.get(out_col) for row in self.output_rows]

        # Try prefix sum transform
        prefix_sum = self._try_prefix_sum(out_col, out_values)
        if prefix_sum:
            return prefix_sum

        # Try prefix count transform
        prefix_count = self._try_prefix_count(out_col, out_values)
        if prefix_count:
            return prefix_count

        # Try sliding window transform

    def _try_window_op(self, out_col: str, in_col: str, in_values: List, out_values: List,
                       window_size: int, op: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Try a specific window operation."""
        window_values = []

        for i in range(len(in_values)):
            start = max(0, i - window_size + 1)
            window = in_values[start:i+1]

            if op == 'sum':
                window_values.append(sum(window))
            elif op == 'mean':
                window_values.append(sum(window) / len(window))

        # Check for linear relationship: out = a * window_values + b
        if len(out_values) >= 2:
            y1, y2 = out_values[0], out_values[1]
            w1, w2 = window_values[0], window_values[1]

            if abs(w2 - w1) < 1e-9:
                if all(abs(out_values[i] - y1) < 1e-9 for i in range(len(out_values))):
                    # Constant
                    return None
                return None

            a = (y2 - y1) / (w2 - w1)
            b = y1 - a * w1

            if all(abs(a * window_values[i] + b - out_values[i]) < 1e-6 for i in range(len(out_values))):
                return {
                    'type': 'sliding_window',
                    'source': in_col,
                    'window_size': window_size,
                    'operation': op,
                    'a': a,
                    'b': b,
                    'output_column': out_col,
                }

def _try_window_op(self, out_col: str, in_col: str, in_values: List, out_values: List,
                       window_size: int, op: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        window_values = []
        for i in range(len(in_values)):
            start = max(0, i - window_size + 1)
            window = in_values[start:i+1]
            window_values.append(sum(window) if op == 'sum' else sum(window) / len(window))

        result = _fit_linear(window_values, out_values, tolerance=1e-6)
        if result:
            return {
                'type': 'sliding_window',
                'source': in_col,
                'window_size': window_size,
                'operation': op,
                'a': result[0],
                'b': result[1],
                'output_column': out_col,
            }

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Dynamic Buffer - A code generator that infers transformations from sample data
and generates modules for streaming data processing.

Supports:
- Per-row transforms (identity, copy, constant, string ops, linear)
- Prefix/cumulative transforms (sum, count, average)
- Sliding window transforms (sum, mean, count over window)
- State-machine sequence labeling (segment IDs, phase flags)
- Neighbor-based filtering (lookahead-dependent filters)
"""

import argparse
import json

     4→and generates modules for streaming data processing.

     6→Supports:

     8→- Prefix/cumulative transforms (sum, count, average)

    24→def parse_csv(filepath: str, delimiter: str = ',') -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    25→    """Parse a CSV/TSV file and return list of row dicts."""
    26→    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    27→        lines = f.read().strip().split('\n')
    28→    if not lines:
    29→        return []
    30→    headers = lines[0].split(delimiter)
    31→    rows = []
    32→    for line in lines[1:]:
    33→        if not line.strip():
    34→            continue
    35→        values = line.split(delimiter)
    36→        rows.append({h: parse_value(values[i]) if i < len(values) else None
    37→                      for i, h in enumerate(headers)})
    38→    return rows
    39→
    40→
    41→def parse_tsv(filepath: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    42→    return parse_csv(filepath, delimiter='\t')

    45→def parse_jsonl(filepath: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    46→    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    47→        return [json.loads(line) for line in f if line.strip()]
    48→
    49→
    50→def parse_json_file(filepath: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    51→    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:

    54→
    55→def parse_value(value: str) -> Any:
    56→    if value == '':
    57→        return None
    58→    if value.lower() == 'true':
    59→        return True
    60→    if value.lower() == 'false':
    61→        return False
    62→    try:
    63→        return int(value)
    64→    except ValueError:
    65→        pass
    66→    try:
    67→        return float(value)
    68→    except ValueError:
    69→        pass
    70→    return value
    71→
    72→
    73→def get_file_extension(filepath: str) -> str:
    74→    return Path(filepath).suffix.lstrip('.').lower()
    75→
    76→
    77→def parse_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[Dict[str, Any]], str]:
    78→    """Parse a data file and return rows and extension."""
    79→    ext = get_file_extension(filepath)
    80→    parsers = {
    81→        'csv': parse_csv,
    82→        'tsv': parse_tsv,
    83→        'jsonl': parse_jsonl,
    84→        'json': parse_json_file,
    85→    }
    86→    if ext not in parsers:

    95→def _fit_linear(x_vals: List, y_vals: List, tolerance: float = 1e-9) -> Optional[Tuple[float, float]]:
    96→    """Fit linear relationship y = a*x + b. Returns (a, b) or None if no fit."""
    97→    if len(x_vals) < 2 or x_vals[0] == x_vals[1]:

    99→    a = (y_vals[1] - y_vals[0]) / (x_vals[1] - x_vals[0])
   100→    b = y_vals[0] - a * x_vals[0]

class PythonCodeGenerator:
    """Generates Python module code from transformation config."""

    def __init__(self, module_name: str, config: Dict, file_ext: str):
        self.module_name = module_name
        self.config = config
        self.file_ext = file_ext

    def generate(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
        return {
            '__init__.py': self._generate_init(),
            'preprocessor.py': self._generate_preprocessor(),
        }

        return '\n'.join([
            'from .preprocessor import DynamicPreprocessor',
            '',
            "__all__ = ['DynamicPreprocessor']",
            '',
        ])

    def _generate_state_init(self) -> List[str]:
        """Generate state variable initialization."""
        lines = []
        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])

        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
            st_type = st.get('type')
            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
                lines.append(f'        self._prefix_sum_{i} = 0.0')
            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
                lines.append(f'        self._prefix_count_{i} = 0')
            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
                window_size = st.get('window_size', 3)
                lines.append(f'        self._window_{i} = deque(maxlen={window_size})')
            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
                initial_state = st.get('initial_state', 1)
                lines.append(f'        self._state_{i} = {repr(initial_state)}')

        return lines

    def _generate_state_methods(self) -> List[str]:
        """Generate state save/restore methods."""
        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])

        lines = [
            '',

            '        return {',
        ]

        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
            st_type = st.get('type')
            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
                lines.append(f'            "prefix_sum_{i}": self._prefix_sum_{i},')
            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
                lines.append(f'            "prefix_count_{i}": self._prefix_count_{i},')
            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
                lines.append(f'            "window_{i}": list(self._window_{i}),')
            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
                lines.append(f'            "state_{i}": self._state_{i},')

        lines.extend([
            '        }',
            '',

        ])

        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
            st_type = st.get('type')
            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
                lines.append(f'        self._prefix_sum_{i} = state.get("prefix_sum_{i}", 0.0)')
            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
                lines.append(f'        self._prefix_count_{i} = state.get("prefix_count_{i}", 0)')
            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
                lines.append(f'        self._window_{i} = deque(state.get("window_{i}", []), maxlen={st.get("window_size", 3)})')
            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
                initial = st.get('initial_state', 1)
                lines.append(f'        self._state_{i} = state.get("state_{i}", {repr(initial)})')

        return lines

    def _generate_filter_logic(self) -> List[str]:
        conditions = self.config.get('filter_conditions', [])
        if not conditions:
            return ['        return True']

        lines = []
        for cond in conditions:
            col = cond['column']
            op = cond['operator']
            val = cond['value']
            val_str = self._py_val_str(val)

            elif op == '==':

            elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):
                comp_ops = {'>': '<=', '>=': '<', '<': '>=', '<=': '>'}

                lines.append("        if val is not None and isinstance(val, (int, float)) and val " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ":")
                lines.append("            return False")
        lines.append("        return True")
        return lines

        for i, nf in enumerate(neighbor_filters):
            nf_type = nf.get('type')
            if nf_type == 'next_row_condition':
                lines.append(f'                keep = keep and self._check_neighbor_filter_{i}(pending_row, row)')

class JavaScriptCodeGenerator:
    """Generates JavaScript module code from transformation config."""

    def __init__(self, module_name: str, config: Dict, file_ext: str):
        self.module_name = module_name
        self.config = config
        self.file_ext = file_ext

    def generate(self) -> str:
        delimiter = ',' if self.file_ext == 'csv' else '\t' if self.file_ext == 'tsv' else None
        has_stateful = bool(self.config.get('stateful_transforms'))
        has_neighbor_filters = bool(self.config.get('neighbor_filters'))

        lines = [
            '/**',
            ' * Dynamic Preprocessor - Streaming data processor with caching support.',
            ' * Supports stateful transforms and neighbor-based filtering.',
            ' */',
            '',
            "const fs = require('fs');",
            "const path = require('path');",
            "const crypto = require('crypto');",
            '',
            'const CONFIG = ' + json.dumps(self.config, indent=2) + ';',
            '',
        ]

        lines.extend([
            'function parseValue(value) {',
            "    if (value === '') return null;",

    def _generate_js_state_methods(self) -> List[str]:
        """Generate JavaScript state save/restore methods."""
        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])

        lines = [
            'function getCurrentState(state) {',
            '    return {',
        ]

        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
            st_type = st.get('type')
            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
                lines.append(f'        prefixSum_{i}: state.prefixSum_{i},')
            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
                lines.append(f'        prefixCount_{i}: state.prefixCount_{i},')
            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
                lines.append(f'        window_{i}: state.window_{i}.slice(),')
            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
                lines.append(f'        state_{i}: state.state_{i},')

        lines.extend([
            '    };',
            '}',
            '',
            'function restoreState(savedState, state) {',
        ])

        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
            st_type = st.get('type')
            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
                lines.append(f'    state.prefixSum_{i} = savedState.prefixSum_{i} || 0.0;')
            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
                lines.append(f'    state.prefixCount_{i} = savedState.prefixCount_{i} || 0;')
            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
                lines.append(f'    state.window_{i} = savedState.window_{i} || [];')
            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
                initial = st.get('initial_state', 1)
                lines.append(f'    state.state_{i} = savedState.state_{i} !== undefined ? savedState.state_{i} : {initial};')

        lines.extend([
            '}',
            '',
        ])

        return lines

    @staticmethod
    def _js_val_str(val: Any) -> str:
        if isinstance(val, bool):
            return 'true' if val else 'false'
        if isinstance(val, str):
            return "'" + val + "'"
        return str(val)

    def _generate_filter_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
        conditions = self.config.get('filter_conditions', [])
        lines = []
        for cond in conditions:
            col = cond['column']
            op = cond['operator']
            val_str = self._js_val_str(cond['value'])
            if op == '!=':
                lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] === " + val_str + ") return false;")
            elif op == '==':
                lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] !== " + val_str + ") return false;")
            elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):
                comp_ops = {'>': '<=', '>=': '<', '<': '>=', '<=': '>'}
                lines.append("    if (typeof row['" + col + "'] === 'number' && row['" + col + "'] " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ") return false;")
        return lines

  1940→def find_sample_files(sample_dir: str) -> Tuple[str, str]:
  1941→    """Find input and output files in sample directory."""
  1942→    sample_path = Path(sample_dir)
  1943→    for ext in ['csv', 'tsv', 'jsonl', 'json']:
  1944→        potential_input = sample_path / ('input.' + ext)
  1945→        potential_output = sample_path / ('output.' + ext)
  1946→        if potential_input.exists() and potential_output.exists():
  1947→            return str(potential_input), str(potential_output)
  1948→    raise ValueError("Could not find matching input/output pair in " + sample_dir)
  1949→
  1950→
  1951→def main():
  1952→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Dynamic Buffer Code Generator')
  1953→    parser.add_argument('module_name', help='Name of the generated module')
  1954→    parser.add_argument('--output', required=True, help='Output directory')
  1955→    parser.add_argument('--sample', required=True, help='Sample directory containing input/output files')
  1956→    parser.add_argument('--python', action='store_true', help='Generate Python module')
  1957→    parser.add_argument('--javascript', action='store_true', help='Generate JavaScript module')
  1958→
  1959→    args = parser.parse_args()
  1960→    if args.python == args.javascript:
  1961→        parser.error("Exactly one of --python or --javascript must be specified")
  1962→
  1963→    input_file, output_file = find_sample_files(args.sample)
  1964→    file_ext = get_file_extension(input_file)
  1965→    input_rows, _ = parse_file(input_file)
  1966→    output_rows, _ = parse_file(output_file)
  1967→
  1968→    inferrer = TransformationInferrer(input_rows, output_rows)
  1969→    config = inferrer.infer()
  1970→
  1971→    output_dir = Path(args.output)
  1972→    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1973→
  1974→    if args.python:
  1975→        generator = PythonCodeGenerator(args.module_name, config, file_ext)
  1976→        files = generator.generate()
  1977→        package_dir = output_dir / args.module_name
  1978→        package_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1979→        for filename, content in files.items():
  1980→            with open(package_dir / filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1981→                f.write(content)
  1982→        print("Generated Python package:", package_dir)
  1983→    elif args.javascript:
  1984→        generator = JavaScriptCodeGenerator(args.module_name, config, file_ext)
  1985→        code = generator.generate()
  1986→        module_dir = output_dir / args.module_name
  1987→        module_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1988→        with open(module_dir / 'index.js', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1989→            f.write(code)
  1990→        print("Generated JavaScript module:", module_dir)
  1991→
  1992→
  1993→if __name__ == '__main__':
  1994→    main()

def _generate_transform_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
        transforms = self.config.get('column_transforms', {})
        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
        stateful_output_cols = {st.get('output_column') for st in stateful_transforms}
        output_cols = self.config.get('output_columns', [])
        lines = []
        for out_col in output_cols:
            if out_col in stateful_output_cols:
                continue

            transform = transforms.get(out_col, {'type': 'unknown'})
            t_type = transform.get('type')
            source = transform.get('source', out_col)
            v = "val_" + out_col.replace('-', '_')
            if t_type in ('identity', 'copy'):
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = row['" + source + "'];")
            elif t_type == 'constant':
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + self._js_val_str(transform['value']) + ";")
            elif t_type in ('strip', 'lower', 'upper', 'strip_lower', 'strip_upper'):
                method_map = {'strip': 'trim', 'lower': 'toLowerCase', 'upper': 'toUpperCase',
                             'strip_lower': 'trim().toLowerCase', 'strip_upper': 'trim().toUpperCase'}
                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = typeof " + v + " === 'string' ? " + v + "." + method_map[t_type] + "() : " + v + ";")
            elif t_type == 'add_prefix':
                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + v + " != null ? '" + transform['prefix'] + "' + String(" + v + ") : null;")
            elif t_type == 'add_suffix':
                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + v + " != null ? String(" + v + ") + '" + transform['suffix'] + "' : null;")
            elif t_type == 'linear':
                a, b = transform['a'], transform['b']
                lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = typeof " + v + " === 'number' ? " + str(a) + " * " + v + " + " + str(b) + " : null;")
            elif t_type != 'stateful':
                lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = row['" + out_col + "'];")
        return lines

    def _generate_stateful_transform_js(self) -> List[str]:
        """Generate JavaScript code for stateful transforms."""
        stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
        lines = []

        for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
            st_type = st.get('type')
            out_col = st.get('output_column')
            source = st.get('source')
            a = st.get('a', 1.0)
            b = st.get('b', 0.0)

            if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
                lines.extend([
                    "    const val_psum_" + str(i) + " = row['" + str(source) + "'];",
                    '    if (typeof val_psum_' + str(i) + ' === "number") {',
                    '        state.prefixSum_' + str(i) + ' += val_psum_' + str(i) + ';',
                    '    }',
                    '    result["' + out_col + '"] = ' + str(a) + ' * state.prefixSum_' + str(i) + ' + ' + str(b) + ';',
                ])
            elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
                condition = st.get('condition')
                lines.append("    const val_pcnt_" + str(i) + " = row['" + str(source) + "'];")

                if condition == 'not_null':
                    lines.extend([
                        '    if (val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' !== null && val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' !== undefined) {',
                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
                        '    }',
                    ])
                elif condition == 'positive':
                    lines.extend([
                        '    if (typeof val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' === "number" && val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' > 0) {',
                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
                        '    }',
                    ])
                elif condition == 'negative':
                    lines.extend([
                        '    if (typeof val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' === "number" && val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' < 0) {',
                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
                        '    }',
                    ])
                elif condition == 'true':
                    lines.extend([
                        '    if (val_pcnt_' + str(i) + ' === true) {',
                        '        state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;',
                        '    }',
                    ])
                else:
                    lines.append('    state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + '++;')

                lines.append('    result["' + out_col + '"] = ' + str(a) + ' * state.prefixCount_' + str(i) + ' + ' + str(b) + ';')

            elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
                window_size = st.get('window_size', 3)
                operation = st.get('operation', 'mean')

                lines.extend([
                    "    const val_win_" + str(i) + " = row['" + str(source) + "'];",
                    '    if (typeof val_win_' + str(i) + ' === "number") {',
                    '        state.window_' + str(i) + '.push(val_win_' + str(i) + ');',
                    '        if (state.window_' + str(i) + '.length > state.windowSize_' + str(i) + ') {',
                    '            state.window_' + str(i) + '.shift();',
                    '        }',
                    '    }',
                ])

                if operation == 'sum':
                    lines.append('    const windowVal_' + str(i) + ' = state.window_' + str(i) + '.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);')
                elif operation == 'mean':
                    lines.extend([
                        '    const windowSum_' + str(i) + ' = state.window_' + str(i) + '.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);',
                        '    const windowVal_' + str(i) + ' = state.window_' + str(i) + '.length > 0 ? windowSum_' + str(i) + ' / state.window_' + str(i) + '.length : 0.0;',
                    ])

                lines.append('    result["' + out_col + '"] = ' + str(a) + ' * windowVal_' + str(i) + ' + ' + str(b) + ';')

            elif st_type == 'state_machine':
                transitions = st.get('transitions', [])

                # Declare the value variable once before the loop
                lines.extend([
                    "    const val_sm_" + str(i) + " = row['" + str(source) + "'];",
                    '    if (typeof val_sm_' + str(i) + ' === "number") {',
                ])

                for t in transitions:
                    t_cond = t.get('condition')
                    if t_cond == 'threshold_cross':
                        threshold = t.get('threshold')
                        direction = t.get('direction')
                        target_state = t.get('target_state')

                        if target_state is not None:
                            if direction == 'up':
                                lines.extend([
                                    '        if (val_sm_' + str(i) + ' >= ' + str(threshold) + ' && state.state_' + str(i) + ' < ' + str(target_state) + ') {',
                                    '            state.state_' + str(i) + ' = ' + str(target_state) + ';',
                                    '        }',
                                ])
                            else:
                                lines.extend([
                                    '        if (val_sm_' + str(i) + ' < ' + str(threshold) + ' && state.state_' + str(i) + ' < ' + str(target_state) + ') {',
                                    '            state.state_' + str(i) + ' = ' + str(target_state) + ';',
                                    '        }',
                                ])

                lines.append('    }')
                lines.append('    result["' + out_col + '"] = state.state_' + str(i) + ';')

        return lines

    def _generate_js_neighbor_filter_logic(self) -> List[str]:
        """Generate JavaScript logic for neighbor-based filtering."""
        neighbor_filters = self.config.get('neighbor_filters', [])

        lines = [
            '                    if (rowCount < startRow) {',
            '                        if (pendingRow !== null && pendingInputIdx === rowCount) {',
            '                            let keep = true;',
        ]

        for i, nf in enumerate(neighbor_filters):
            nf_type = nf.get('type')
            if nf_type == 'next_row_condition':
                col = nf.get('column')
                val = self._js_val_str(nf.get('value'))
                lines.append(f'                            keep = keep && (row["{col}"] !== {val});')
            elif nf_type == 'consecutive_duplicate':
                col = nf.get('column')
                lines.append(f'                            keep = keep && (prevRow === null || prevRow["{col}"] !== pendingRow["{col}"]);')

        lines.extend([
            '                            if (keep && shouldKeepRow(pendingRow)) {',
            '                                const transformed = transformRow(pendingRow, self.state);',
            '                                if (cachePath) {',
            '                                    currentCacheRows.push(transformed);',
            '                                    bufferCount++;',
            '                                    if (bufferCount >= self.buffer) {',
            '                                        cacheState.processedRows = rowCount;',
            '                                        cacheState.rows = currentCacheRows;',
            '                                        saveCache(cachePath, cacheState, self.state);',
            '                                        currentCacheRows.length = 0;',
            '                                        bufferCount = 0;',
            '                                    }',
            '                                }',
            '                                yield transformed;',
            '                            }',
            '                            pendingRow = null;',
            '                            pendingInputIdx = null;',
            '                        }',
            '                        prevRow = row;',
            '                        rowCount++;',
            '                        continue;',
            '                    }',
            '',
            '                    if (pendingRow !== null) {',
            '                        let keep = true;',
        ])

        for i, nf in enumerate(neighbor_filters):
            nf_type = nf.get('type')
            if nf_type == 'next_row_condition':
                col = nf.get('column')
                val = self._js_val_str(nf.get('value'))
                lines.append(f'                        keep = keep && (row["{col}"] !== {val});')
            elif nf_type == 'consecutive_duplicate':
                col = nf.get('column')
                lines.append(f'                        keep = keep && (prevRow === null || prevRow["{col}"] !== pendingRow["{col}"]);')

        lines.extend([
            '                        if (keep && shouldKeepRow(pendingRow)) {',
            '                            const transformed = transformRow(pendingRow, self.state);',
            '                            if (cachePath) {',
            '                                currentCacheRows.push(transformed);',
            '                                bufferCount++;',
            '                                if (bufferCount >= self.buffer) {',
            '                                    cacheState.processedRows = rowCount;',
            '                                    cacheState.rows = currentCacheRows;',
            '                                    saveCache(cachePath, cacheState, self.state);',
            '                                    currentCacheRows.length = 0;',
            '                                    bufferCount = 0;',
            '                                }',
            '                            }',
            '                            yield transformed;',
            '                        }',
            '                        pendingRow = null;',
            '                    }',
            '',
            '                    pendingRow = row;',
            '                    pendingInputIdx = rowCount;',
            '                    prevRow = row;',
            '                    rowCount++;',
        ])

        return lines


# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

def find_sample_files(sample_dir: str) -> Tuple[str, str]:
    """Find input and output files in sample directory."""
    sample_path = Path(sample_dir)
    for ext in ['csv', 'tsv', 'jsonl', 'json']:
        potential_input = sample_path / ('input.' + ext)
        potential_output = sample_path / ('output.' + ext)
        if potential_input.exists() and potential_output.exists():
            return str(potential_input), str(potential_output)
    raise ValueError("Could not find matching input/output pair in " + sample_dir)


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Dynamic Buffer Code Generator')
    parser.add_argument('module_name', help='Name of the generated module')
    parser.add_argument('--output', required=True, help='Output directory')
    parser.add_argument('--sample', required=True, help='Sample directory containing input/output files')
    parser.add_argument('--python', action='store_true', help='Generate Python module')
    parser.add_argument('--javascript', action='store_true', help='Generate JavaScript module')

    args = parser.parse_args()
    if args.python == args.javascript:
        parser.error("Exactly one of --python or --javascript must be specified")

    input_file, output_file = find_sample_files(args.sample)
    file_ext = get_file_extension(input_file)
    input_rows, _ = parse_file(input_file)
    output_rows, _ = parse_file(output_file)

    inferrer = TransformationInferrer(input_rows, output_rows)
    config = inferrer.infer()

    output_dir = Path(args.output)
    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    if args.python:
        generator = PythonCodeGenerator(args.module_name, config, file_ext)
        files = generator.generate()
        package_dir = output_dir / args.module_name
        package_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        for filename, content in files.items():
            with open(package_dir / filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                f.write(content)
        print("Generated Python package:", package_dir)
    elif args.javascript:
        generator = JavaScriptCodeGenerator(args.module_name, config, file_ext)
        code = generator.generate()
        module_dir = output_dir / args.module_name
        module_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(module_dir / 'index.js', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            f.write(code)
        print("Generated JavaScript module:", module_dir)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

Now I have a complete view of both generators. Let me provide a comprehensive analysis of the duplicated patterns.

## Analysis of Duplicated Patterns in `/workspace/dynamic_buffer.py`

### 1. `_py_val_str` vs `_js_val_str` Functions

**Python version (lines 1021-1027):**
```python
@staticmethod
def _py_val_str(val: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(val, bool):
        return 'True' if val else 'False'
    if isinstance(val, str):
        return "'" + val + "'"
    return str(val)
```

**JavaScript version (lines 1677-1683):**
```python
@staticmethod
def _js_val_str(val: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(val, bool):
        return 'true' if val else 'false'
    if isinstance(val, str):
        return "'" + val + "'"
    return str(val)
```

**Structural Similarity:** These functions are **nearly identical**. The only difference is the boolean literal output: Python uses `True`/`False` (capitalized) while JavaScript uses `true`/`false` (lowercase). Both have the same:
- 3-way conditional structure
- String quoting with single quotes
- Fallback to `str(val)` for numbers

---

### 2. `_generate_filter_logic` vs `_generate_filter_logic_js`

**Python version (lines 956-982):**
```python
def _generate_filter_logic(self) -> List[str]:
    conditions = self.config.get('filter_conditions', [])
    if not conditions:
        return ['        return True']

    lines = []
    for cond in conditions:
        col = cond['column']
        op = cond['operator']
        val = cond['value']
        val_str = self._py_val_str(val)
        if op == '!=':
            if isinstance(val, bool):
                lines.append("        if row.get('" + col + "') is " + val_str + ":")
            else:
                lines.append("        if row.get('" + col + "') == " + val_str + ":")
            lines.append("            return False")
        elif op == '==':
            lines.append("        if row.get('" + col + "') != " + val_str + ":")
            lines.append("            return False")
        elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):
            comp_ops = {'>': '<=', '>=': '<', '<': '>=', '<=': '>'}
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + col + "')")
            lines.append("        if val is not None and isinstance(val, (int, float)) and val " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ":")
            lines.append("            return False")
    lines.append("        return True")
    return lines
```

**JavaScript version (lines 1685-1699):**
```python
def _generate_filter_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
    conditions = self.config.get('filter_conditions', [])
    lines = []
    for cond in conditions:
        col = cond['column']
        op = cond['operator']
        val_str = self._js_val_str(cond['value'])
        if op == '!=':
            lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] === " + val_str + ") return false;")
        elif op == '==':
            lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] !== " + val_str + ") return false;")
        elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):
            comp_ops = {'>': '<=', '>=': '<', '<': '>=', '<=': '>'}
            lines.append("    if (typeof row['" + col + "'] === 'number' && row['" + col + "'] " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ") return false;")
    return lines
```

**Structural Similarity:** **Very high**. Key differences:
- Python uses `row.get()` vs JavaScript uses `row['col']` direct access
- Python returns `True`/`False` vs JavaScript returns `true`/`false`
- Python has special handling for boolean comparison with `is` keyword
- Python checks `isinstance(val, (int, float))` vs JavaScript uses `typeof === 'number'`
- Same `comp_ops` dictionary for inverting comparison operators
- Same control flow structure iterating over conditions

---

### 3. `_generate_transform_logic` vs `_generate_transform_logic_js`

**Python version (lines 1029-1065):**
```python
def _generate_transform_logic(self) -> List[str]:
    transforms = self.config.get('column_transforms', {})
    stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
    stateful_output_cols = {st.get('output_column') for st in stateful_transforms}
    output_cols = self.config.get('output_columns', [])
    lines = []

    for out_col in output_cols:
        if out_col in stateful_output_cols:
            continue  # Will be handled separately

        transform = transforms.get(out_col, {'type': 'unknown'})
        t_type = transform.get('type')
        source = transform.get('source', out_col)
        if t_type in ('identity', 'copy'):
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = row.get('" + source + "')")
        elif t_type == 'constant':
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = " + self._py_val_str(transform['value']))
        elif t_type in ('strip', 'lower', 'upper', 'strip_lower', 'strip_upper'):
            method_map = {'strip': 'strip', 'lower': 'lower', 'upper': 'upper',
                         'strip_lower': 'strip().lower', 'strip_upper': 'strip().upper'}
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = val." + method_map[t_type] + "() if isinstance(val, str) else val")
        elif t_type == 'add_prefix':
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = '" + transform['prefix'] + "' + str(val) if val is not None else None")
        elif t_type == 'add_suffix':
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = str(val) + '" + transform['suffix'] + "' if val is not None else None")
        elif t_type == 'linear':
            a, b = transform['a'], transform['b']
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = " + str(a) + " * val + " + str(b) + " if isinstance(val, (int, float)) else None")
        elif t_type != 'stateful':
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = row.get('" + out_col + "')")

    return lines
```

**JavaScript version (lines 1701-1736):**
```python
def _generate_transform_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
    transforms = self.config.get('column_transforms', {})
    stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
    stateful_output_cols = {st.get('output_column') for st in stateful_transforms}
    output_cols = self.config.get('output_columns', [])
    lines = []
    for out_col in output_cols:
        if out_col in stateful_output_cols:
            continue

        transform = transforms.get(out_col, {'type': 'unknown'})
        t_type = transform.get('type')
        source = transform.get('source', out_col)
        v = "val_" + out_col.replace('-', '_')
        if t_type in ('identity', 'copy'):
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = row['" + source + "'];")
        elif t_type == 'constant':
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + self._js_val_str(transform['value']) + ";")
        elif t_type in ('strip', 'lower', 'upper', 'strip_lower', 'strip_upper'):
            method_map = {'strip': 'trim', 'lower': 'toLowerCase', 'upper': 'toUpperCase',
                         'strip_lower': 'trim().toLowerCase', 'strip_upper': 'trim().toUpperCase'}
            lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = typeof " + v + " === 'string' ? " + v + "." + method_map[t_type] + "() : " + v + ";")
        elif t_type == 'add_prefix':
            lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + v + " != null ? '" + transform['prefix'] + "' + String(" + v + ") : null;")
        elif t_type == 'add_suffix':
            lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = " + v + " != null ? String(" + v + ") + '" + transform['suffix'] + "' : null;")
        elif t_type == 'linear':
            a, b = transform['a'], transform['b']
            lines.append("    const " + v + " = row['" + source + "'];")
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = typeof " + v + " === 'number' ? " + str(a) + " * " + v + " + " + str(b) + " : null;")
        elif t_type != 'stateful':
            lines.append("    result['" + out_col + "'] = row['" + out_col + "'];")
    return lines
```

**Structural Similarity:** **Extremely high**. Both functions:
- Have identical setup (extract transforms, stateful_transforms, output_cols)
- Skip stateful output columns with same logic
- Handle the same transform types: `identity/copy`, `constant`, `strip/lower/upper/strip_lower/strip_upper`, `add_prefix`, `add_suffix`, `linear`
- Use a `method_map` dictionary for string operations (only method names differ: `strip` vs `trim`, `lower` vs `toLowerCase`)

**Key differences:**
- JavaScript uses unique variable names (`val_colname`) to avoid collisions; Python reuses `val`
- Python uses `row.get()` vs JavaScript uses `row['col']`
- Python uses `isinstance(val, str)` vs JavaScript uses `typeof === 'string'`
- Python uses `is not None` vs JavaScript uses `!= null`
- JavaScript appends `;` to statements

---

### 4. `_generate_stateful_transform_logic` vs `_generate_stateful_transform_js`

**Python version (lines 1067-1167):**

**JavaScript version (lines 1738-1848):**
```python
def _generate_stateful_transform_js(self) -> List[str]:
    """Generate JavaScript code for stateful transforms."""
    stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])
    lines = []

    for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):

**Structural Similarity:** **Extremely high**. Both functions handle:
- `prefix_sum`: Accumulate values with optional scaling (a, b coefficients)
- `prefix_count`: Count rows based on conditions (`not_null`, `positive`, `negative`, `true`, default)
- `sliding_window`: Maintain fixed-size window with `sum` or `mean` operations
- `state_machine`: Threshold-based state transitions with `up`/`down` directions

**Key differences:**
- State variable naming: Python uses `self._prefix_sum_0` vs JavaScript uses `state.prefixSum_0`
- Type checking: Python `isinstance(val, (int, float))` vs JavaScript `typeof === "number"`
- JavaScript uses unique variable names per iteration (`val_psum_0`, `val_pcnt_0`, etc.) to avoid scoping issues

### 5. State Initialization Patterns

**Python `_generate_state_init` (lines 895-913):**

def _generate_state_init(self) -> List[str]:
    """Generate state variable initialization."""
    lines = []
    stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])

    for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
        st_type = st.get('type')
        if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
            lines.append(f'        self._prefix_sum_{i} = 0.0')
        elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
            lines.append(f'        self._prefix_count_{i} = 0')
        elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
            window_size = st.get('window_size', 3)
            lines.append(f'        self._window_{i} = deque(maxlen={window_size})')
        elif st_type == 'state_machine':
            initial_state = st.get('initial_state', 1)
            lines.append(f'        self._state_{i} = {repr(initial_state)}')

    return lines
```

**JavaScript inline initialization (lines 1480-1498):**

    elif st_type == 'prefix_count':

### 6. State Save/Restore Methods

**Python `_generate_state_methods` (lines 915-954):**

**JavaScript `_generate_js_state_methods` (lines 1631-1675):**

- `restoreState(savedState, state)` - restores from saved object

**Structural Similarity:** **Extremely high**. Both iterate over `stateful_transforms` and handle the same types with equivalent logic.

---

### 7. Neighbor Filter Logic

**Python `_generate_neighbor_filter_logic` (lines 1263-1333)**
**JavaScript `_generate_js_neighbor_filter_logic` (lines 1850-1933)**

**Structural Similarity:** **Extremely high**. Both:
- Handle `next_row_condition` and `consecutive_duplicate` filter types
- Use the same pending row mechanism
- Same cache handling pattern
- Same yield of transformed rows

---

## Summary Table

| Function Pair | Python Lines | JS Lines | Similarity Level |
|--------------|--------------|----------|------------------|
| `_py_val_str` vs `_js_val_str` | 1021-1027 | 1677-1683 | Nearly identical (bool capitalization only) |
| `_generate_filter_logic` vs `_generate_filter_logic_js` | 956-982 | 1685-1699 | Very high (same structure, different syntax) |
| `_generate_transform_logic` vs `_generate_transform_logic_js` | 1029-1065 | 1701-1736 | Extremely high (same transform types, same logic) |
| `_generate_stateful_transform_logic` vs `_generate_stateful_transform_js` | 1067-1167 | 1738-1848 | Extremely high (same state types, same operations) |
| State initialization | 895-913 | 1480-1498 | Extremely high (same loop, same defaults) |
| State save/restore | 915-954 | 1631-1675 | Extremely high (same pattern) |
| Neighbor filter logic | 1263-1333 | 1850-1933 | Extremely high (same mechanism) |

---

## Conclusion

The `PythonCodeGenerator` (starting at line 720) and `JavaScriptCodeGenerator` (starting at line 1340) contain **extensive structural duplication**. The primary differences are:
1. **Syntax**: Python uses `self.`, `isinstance()`, `row.get()` vs JavaScript uses `state.`, `typeof`, `row['col']`
2. **Naming conventions**: `prefix_sum_0` vs `prefixSum_0` (snake_case vs camelCase)
3. **Type checking**: `isinstance(val, (int, float))` vs `typeof val === "number"`
4. **Variable scoping**: JavaScript needs unique variable names per block to avoid collision

The core logic, control flow, and algorithmic structure are **identical** between both generators.

    for cond in conditions:
        col = cond['column']
        op = cond['operator']
        val = cond['value']
        val_str = self._py_val_str(val)
        if op == '!=':
            if isinstance(val, bool):
                lines.append("        if row.get('" + col + "') is " + val_str + ":")
            else:
                lines.append("        if row.get('" + col + "') == " + val_str + ":")
            lines.append("            return False")
        elif op == '==':
            lines.append("        if row.get('" + col + "') != " + val_str + ":")
            lines.append("            return False")
        elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):
            comp_ops = {'>': '<=', '>=': '<', '<': '>=', '<=': '>'}
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + col + "')")
            lines.append("        if val is not None and isinstance(val, (int, float)) and val " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ":")
            lines.append("            return False")
    lines.append("        return True")
    return lines
```

**JavaScript version (lines 1685-1699):**

def _generate_filter_logic_js(self) -> List[str]:
    conditions = self.config.get('filter_conditions', [])

    for cond in conditions:

        elif op == '==':
            lines.append("    if (row['" + col + "'] !== " + val_str + ") return false;")
        elif op in ('>', '>=', '<', '<='):

            lines.append("    if (typeof row['" + col + "'] === 'number' && row['" + col + "'] " + comp_ops[op] + " " + val_str + ") return false;")
    return lines
```

def _generate_transform_logic(self) -> List[str]:
    transforms = self.config.get('column_transforms', {})

    output_cols = self.config.get('output_columns', [])
    lines = []

    for out_col in output_cols:
        if out_col in stateful_output_cols:
            continue  # Will be handled separately

        transform = transforms.get(out_col, {'type': 'unknown'})
        t_type = transform.get('type')
        source = transform.get('source', out_col)
        if t_type in ('identity', 'copy'):
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = row.get('" + source + "')")
        elif t_type == 'constant':
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = " + self._py_val_str(transform['value']))
        elif t_type in ('strip', 'lower', 'upper', 'strip_lower', 'strip_upper'):
            method_map = {'strip': 'strip', 'lower': 'lower', 'upper': 'upper',
                         'strip_lower': 'strip().lower', 'strip_upper': 'strip().upper'}
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = val." + method_map[t_type] + "() if isinstance(val, str) else val")
        elif t_type == 'add_prefix':
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = '" + transform['prefix'] + "' + str(val) if val is not None else None")
        elif t_type == 'add_suffix':
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = str(val) + '" + transform['suffix'] + "' if val is not None else None")
        elif t_type == 'linear':
            a, b = transform['a'], transform['b']
            lines.append("        val = row.get('" + source + "')")
            lines.append("        result['" + out_col + "'] = " + str(a) + " * val + " + str(b) + " if isinstance(val, (int, float)) else None")
        elif t_type != 'stateful':

    return lines

def _generate_state_init(self) -> List[str]:
    """Generate state variable initialization."""
    lines = []
    stateful_transforms = self.config.get('stateful_transforms', [])

    for i, st in enumerate(stateful_transforms):
        st_type = st.get('type')
        if st_type == 'prefix_sum':
            lines.append(f'        self._prefix_sum_{i} = 0.0')
        elif st_type == 'prefix_count':
            lines.append(f'        self._prefix_count_{i} = 0')
        elif st_type == 'sliding_window':
            window_size = st.get('window_size', 3)
            lines.append(f'        self._window_{i} = deque(maxlen={window_size})')
        elif st_type == 'state_machine':
            initial_state = st.get('initial_state', 1)
            lines.append(f'        self._state_{i} = {repr(initial_state)}')

    return lines
